title: cache_pattern silently drops compile errors — correct for post-validation path
description: In cache_pattern and cache_glob_pattern, regex compilation errors are silently ignored (if let Ok(re) = ...). A reviewer might flag these as hiding errors. They are correct: build_regex_cache runs only after validate_script has already rejected the script if any pattern was invalid. The Ok-only arm is the structurally reachable path; the Err arm is dead code under the invariant that compile() always calls validate_script first. Adding an expect() or unwrap() here would be equally correct but would abort on the (currently impossible) case of validate and cache using different anchoring logic.
